questions.question: Write a C function to insert a new node at the beginning of a singly linked list. The function should take the head of the list and the value of the new node as arguments, and return the new head of the updated list.
questions.hint: You can assume that the linked list nodes are in a struct called 'Node', and that the head of the list is initialized to NULL.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a singly linked list and a doubly linked list. Provide an example of when you might use each.
questions.hint: You should use a diagram or flowchart to illustrate your answer.

questions.question: Write a C function to check if a given list is empty. The function should take the head of the list as an argument, and return 1 if the list is empty and 0 otherwise.
questions.hint: You can assume that the linked list nodes are in a struct called 'Node', and that the head of the list is initialized to NULL.
